_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL
name: Aether Tickets
author: aether@example.com
description: Aether Tickets — Discord ticket bot with optional web dashboard. Auto-clones from GitHub on install, auto-pulls updates on every restart. No file uploads needed.

installation.script:
#!/bin/sh
# ============================================================
# Aether Tickets — Pterodactyl installation script
# Runs ONCE when the server is first created.
# ============================================================
apk add --no-cache git curl python3 py3-pip

cd /mnt/server

GIT_REPO="${GIT_REPO:-https://github.com/Shaf2665/Aether_Tickets.git}"
GIT_BRANCH="${GIT_BRANCH:-main}"

echo "[install] Cloning ${GIT_REPO} (branch: ${GIT_BRANCH})..."
git clone --depth=1 --branch "${GIT_BRANCH}" "${GIT_REPO}" .

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[install] ERROR: git clone failed. Check GIT_REPO and GIT_BRANCH."
  exit 1
fi

echo "[install] Installing Python dependencies..."
pip3 install -U pip --quiet
pip3 install -U -r requirements.txt --quiet

echo ""
echo "============================================================"
echo " Installation complete!"
echo "============================================================"
echo " Next steps:"
echo "  1. Go to the Startup tab and fill in your Discord Bot Token"
echo "  2. Click START -- the bot will launch automatically"
echo "  3. In your Discord server, run: /setup start"
echo "============================================================"

variables.name: 📖 QUICK SETUP GUIDE — READ FIRST
variables.description: STEP 1 — Create your Discord bot: Go to https://discord.com/developers/applications → New Application → Bot tab → Reset Token → copy the token and paste it into 'Discord Bot Token' below. Enable all 3 Privileged Gateway Intents (Presence, Server Members, Message Content). | STEP 2 — Invite the bot to your server: In the Developer Portal → OAuth2 → URL Generator. Tick scopes: bot + applications.commands. Tick permissions: Manage Channels, Manage Roles, Read Messages, Send Messages, Embed Links, Read Message History, Manage Messages. Open the generated URL and invite the bot. | STEP 3 — Fill in 'Discord Bot Token' below, then click SAVE and START the server. | STEP 4 — In your Discord server, run the slash command: /setup start — and follow the prompts to configure the ticket panel. | That's it! The bot is ready. For the web dashboard, also fill in the WEB DASHBOARD section below.

variables.name: 🔴 REQUIRED — Discord Bot Token
variables.description: Paste your Discord bot token here. Get it from: https://discord.com/developers/applications → select your app → Bot → Reset Token. WARNING: Never share this token with anyone.

variables.name: 🟡 OPTIONAL — Guild ID (faster slash command sync)
variables.description: Your Discord server ID. If set, slash commands appear instantly in that server. Without it, commands may take up to 1 hour to appear globally. To get your server ID: enable Developer Mode in Discord (User Settings → Advanced → Developer Mode), then right-click your server name → Copy Server ID.

variables.name: ━━━━━━━━━━ 🌐 WEB DASHBOARD (optional) ━━━━━━━━━━
variables.description: The web dashboard lets you view and manage tickets from a browser. To enable it: (1) Set Launch Mode to 'both' below. (2) In Discord Developer Portal → your app → OAuth2, add a Redirect URL matching your access URL exactly — e.g. http://free-01.kovaihost.cloud:2000/auth/callback (HTTP) or https://tickets.yourdomain.com/auth/callback (HTTPS if behind a proxy). (3) Fill in the 4 fields below. (4) If using HTTPS, also enable 'Behind Reverse Proxy' in the SSL section. Leave all fields blank and set Launch Mode to 'bot' if you only want the Discord bot.

variables.name: Discord OAuth Client ID
variables.description: From Discord Developer Portal → your app → OAuth2 → Client ID. Required for web dashboard login.

variables.name: Discord OAuth Client Secret
variables.description: From Discord Developer Portal → your app → OAuth2 → Client Secret. Required for web dashboard login. Keep this value private — do not share it.

variables.name: Flask Secret Key
variables.description: A random string used to secure login sessions. Generate one at: https://it-tools.tech/token-generator — copy a 64-char token and paste it here.

variables.name: Discord OAuth Redirect URI
variables.description: The callback URL for Discord login. Must be: http://YOUR-NODE-ADDRESS:YOUR-PORT/auth/callback — replace with your actual node address and port shown in the panel. Example: http://free-01.kovaihost.cloud:2000/auth/callback — This EXACT URL must also be added in Discord Developer Portal → OAuth2 → Redirects.

variables.name: ━━━━━━━━━━ 🔒 SSL / HTTPS (optional) ━━━━━━━━━━
variables.description: Enable these only if your Pterodactyl node is behind a reverse proxy (Nginx, Caddy, Cloudflare) that terminates HTTPS. If your dashboard URL starts with https://, set 'Behind Reverse Proxy' to true and update your Redirect URI to use https://. If you are using plain http://, leave both disabled.

variables.name: Behind Reverse Proxy (enables HTTPS support)
variables.description: Set to true if your node uses Nginx/Caddy/Cloudflare to serve the dashboard over HTTPS. This enables ProxyFix so Flask trusts X-Forwarded-Proto headers and generates correct https:// URLs. Leave false for plain http:// access.

variables.name: ━━━━━━━━━━ ⚙️ LAUNCH SETTINGS ━━━━━━━━━━
variables.description: Control what gets launched when the server starts.

variables.name: Launch Mode
variables.description: What to run when the server starts: 'bot' = Discord bot only (simplest, recommended to start). 'both' = Discord bot + web dashboard (requires web dashboard fields above). 'web' = Web dashboard only.

variables.name: Auto Update on Restart
variables.description: Automatically pull the latest code from GitHub every time the server restarts. Enabled = always update (recommended). Disabled = keep current files.

variables.name: ━━━━━━━━━━ 🔧 ADVANCED (do not change unless you know what you're doing) ━━━━━━━━━━
variables.description: These are pre-filled with correct defaults. Only change them if you have forked the repository or need a custom setup.

variables.name: Git Repository URL
variables.description: The GitHub repository to clone and pull updates from. Pre-filled with the official Aether Tickets repo. Only change this if you have forked the repository.

variables.name: Git Branch
variables.description: Which branch to track. 'main' = stable releases. Only change if you want to run a development/beta branch.

variables.name: Database Path
variables.description: Path to the SQLite database file. The default 'tickets.db' saves it in /home/container which is persistent across restarts. Do not change this.

variables.name: Flask Environment
variables.description: Set to 'production' for normal use. Only change to 'development' if you are debugging (this shows detailed error pages — do not use in production).
